title: podolepis aristata subsp. auriculata
intro: Podolepis aristata subsp. auriculata, also known as the Golden Bells or Stinking Roger, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Podolepis aristata subsp. auriculata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
